name: Iwate Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the sea, the sky, and a vigorous hopeful future
purity and the snows of the Ou Mountains

description: A blue field charged at the centre with a white symbol formed from a stylised hiragana 'i' (い), giving a wide outer pair of curves embracing a smaller central form.
history: Iwate's prefectural symbol was selected on 16 March 1965 and the flag bearing it on a blue field was adopted alongside it. The mark is a stylised version of the hiragana 'i' (い), the first letter of the prefecture's name.

features.symbolism: the deep blue of the Pacific Ocean off the Sanriku Coast

features.role: stylised hiragana 'i' (い) for 'Iwate', drawn as a wide opened pair of brackets containing a smaller central form
features.symbolism: the hiragana 'i' for Iwate, rendered to evoke the wide spreading of the prefecture and the union of its people; the inner element conveys harmony and forward motion.
